key_insight: Two-dimensional dodecagonal quasicrystals store cyclic-shear memory through nested, reversible phason-like tile rearrangements that act as bistable hysterons.
engineering_relevance: This work suggests a route to design materials with programmable mechanical memory by exploiting quasicrystal-specific switching units rather than relying on amorphous disorder.
method: Athermal quasistatic shear simulations of 2D dodecagonal quasicrystals
Cyclic loading with varying amplitudes to probe return-point memory
Microscopic tracking of reversible plastic events and tile rearrangements
evidence_signal: The abstract reports hierarchical nested hysteresis loops below yield, persistent shear bands above yield, and a contrast with a periodic approximant that lacks the bistable rearrangements.
watch_item: The memory mechanism is demonstrated in idealized simulation conditions, so experimental realization and robustness under finite temperature or defects remain to be verified.
paper_title: How Quasicrystals Remember: Hierarchical Memory Under Cyclic Shear
